frameworks.name: structural equation modeling
frameworks.one_line: decomposes striatal effects on cognition into direct vs fronto-parietal-mediated paths

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: ventral-dorsal striatum and fronto-parietal cortex measured at rest

frameworks.name: cortico-cortical strengthening hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: striatum contributes to executive function by reinforcing cortical connectivity

core_question: How does striatal connectivity contribute to executive function across childhood development?
key_assumptions: resting-state functional connectivity indexes functional coupling relevant to cognition
executive function is a compound of working memory, inhibition, and set-shifting
indirect effects via fronto-parietal pathways can be statistically separated from direct effects
manipulations: inter-individual variation in ventral-dorsal striatum connectivity
key_findings: striatal connectivity shows indirect effect on executive function via fronto-parietal connectivity
striatal connectivity also shows a direct effect on executive function
fronto-parietal influence on cognition is age-invariant across childhood
striatal connectivity tracks developmental changes in cognitive ability
interpretations: striatum promotes cognitive development by strengthening cortico-cortical connectivity
striatum has a specific role in cognitive plasticity during development
